clarified_problem_statement: Rewrite scene_expansion_items for all 20 blind-box scenes so the pool represents medium-scale scene fragments rather than large furniture/storage objects, generic containers, or tiny cards/labels.

confirmed_target_users.name: Prompt maintainer
needs: Stable content rules that prevent future drift back to oversized or undersized objects.
Clear pool boundaries among core_items, support_items, visible_small_items, and scene_expansion_items.
pain_points: Large furniture terms create placement burden.
Container and card alternatives blur pool semantics.

confirmed_target_users.name: Game content extraction tool maintainer
needs: Data changes that preserve existing runtime contracts and tests.
Concrete acceptance checks for data quality.
pain_points: Changing pool semantics can accidentally require UI or runtime logic changes.
Manual list editing can break uniqueness or count invariants.

confirmed_features.name: Medium-scale scene fragment content model
confirmed_features.description: Define scene_expansion_items around activity results, medium task surfaces, setup surfaces, grouped arrays, and complete/partial outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: The spec defines five allowed item families for scene_expansion_items.
The spec defines scale boundaries that reject both oversized furniture and tiny card/label subjects.
The model explains how scene_expansion_items differs from core_items, support_items, and visible_small_items.
edge_cases: Information terms like label or price can appear only when upgraded into a larger board, surface, array, or outcome.
Some medium carrier objects may remain only as rare exceptions, not the pool backbone.

confirmed_features.name: All-scene data rewrite
confirmed_features.description: Rewrite scene_expansion_items for all 20 scenes while preserving 50 unique items per scene.
acceptance_criteria: Every scene has exactly 50 unique scene_expansion_items.
No scene_expansion_items list uses large furniture/storage terms as the primary pattern.
No scene_expansion_items list uses tiny card/label/ticket terms as standalone primary subjects.
edge_cases: Chinese synonyms may imply the same disallowed scale even when exact blacklist terms do not match.
Duplicates may arise after replacing similar large terms.

confirmed_features.name: Content-quality validation
confirmed_features.description: Extend or add tests/checks that guard structural invariants and the new content-quality constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Quality checks detect large furniture/storage subjects in scene_expansion_items.
Quality checks detect tiny card/label/ticket subjects used as standalone scene_expansion_items.
edge_cases: Allowed upgraded forms such as 价格板 or 记录板 must not be falsely rejected.
Existing blocked patterns for light/reflection/tiny traces remain enforced.

confirmed_features.name: Documentation synchronization
confirmed_features.description: Update stable maintainer documentation if the implementation accepts the new scene_expansion_items semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Game content extraction/agents.md states the medium-scale scene fragment rule succinctly.
agents.md, README.md, and .gitignore are checked and updated only if necessary.
Documentation remains concise and does not duplicate full item lists.
edge_cases: 主图 第一步.md should not change unless category IDs or category semantics change.
README.md should not change unless user-facing behavior changes.

non_functional_requirements.details: Data rewrite must preserve runtime compatibility and draw-history keys.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_criteria: All existing blind-box content model tests pass.

non_functional_requirements.details: New content rules must be simple enough for future list maintenance.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_criteria: Documentation contains concise allowed families, disallowed subject categories, and examples.

non_functional_requirements.details: Generated item names must remain clear Chinese concrete objects that are first-eye visible and independently circleable.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_criteria: Manual review of pilot scenes confirms no ambiguous, too-large, or too-small primary subjects.

in_scope: Add or extend data quality tests for the new scale boundaries.
out_of_scope: Changing scene IDs, category names, or runtime compatibility mapping.
Changing tkinter UI, draw history behavior, animal pools, expression pools, image fetching, or renaming features.
constraints: Do not rely on light, reflection, transparency, glowing, thin-line, or micro-trace differences.

key_assumptions: User accepts the latest brainstorm direction: medium-scale scene fragments.
The runtime mapping can stay unchanged even though scene_expansion_items names become less furniture-like.
The implementation can use manual or generated list editing as long as tests enforce count and uniqueness.

discussion_log.agent_prompt: Brainstorm scene_expansion_items alternatives that are smaller than carts/cabinets/racks.
discussion_log.user_response: Boxes and baskets blur pool boundaries.
Avoid too-small content such as small cards.
Current optimization is acceptable; write as spec handoff.
